channel.title: Chandra Kanojia Field Notes
channel.description: Enterprise AI, operating models, workflow, data, controlled autonomy, and value realization.

item.title: Aligning Capabilities Around the Constraint: The VECTOR Operating Thesis
item.description: Transformation creates value when the enterprise aligns its capabilities around the constraint that matters most, then carries that alignment through evidence, adoption, and scale.

item.title: When Growth Is Still Tied to Headcount, AI Has Not Changed the Operating Model
item.description: Task productivity is not operating leverage. The real test is whether the binding constraint moved and whether the economics of work changed.

item.title: Why AI Transformation in Regulated Enterprises Is an Operating-Model Problem
item.description: The hard part is not proving that a model can perform. It is redesigning the workflow, evidence, decisions, controls, and adoption around it.

item.title: Controlled Autonomy Is the Enterprise AI Product
item.description: Autonomy becomes valuable when the organization designs the boundaries, evidence, escalation, and accountability around it.

item.title: The Data Problem Hiding Under Every AI Strategy
item.description: Enterprises often have data. What they lack is the context, ownership, evidence, and workflow access required for responsible action.

item.title: The CRM Trap: When Systems of Record Never Become Systems of Action
item.description: A platform that records the work but does not improve how the work moves leaves operating value trapped.
